page: Microsoft Azure D160als_v7 is a compute-focused virtual machine instance belonging to the Dalsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 160 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 80 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with an x86_64 architecture. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, including VNNI and BF16 vector extensions, alongside cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

The instance is configured with 320.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This lower memory-to-CPU ratio represents a design tradeoff optimized for compute-heavy workloads rather than memory-intensive databases. There is no local NVMe storage bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable for external block storage attachment. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

In benchmark evaluations, the D160als_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached read speeds reaching 14,528.43 GB/sec and uncached write speeds at 210.18 GB/sec. The platform also excels in data processing, showing top-tier throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving. These characteristics make the instance suitable for compute-heavy tasks, parallel batch processing, web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D160als_v7 is an x86_64 compute-focused virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It delivers 160 dedicated vCPUs across 80 physical cores, paired with 320.0 GB of system memory. This configuration results in a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, prioritizing raw compute density over memory capacity. The instance lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage. In benchmark testing, the D160als_v7 achieves top-tier results in multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and Redis operations. It is designed for compute-intensive workloads, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D160als_v7 is a compute-focused virtual machine featuring 160 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 320 GB of RAM, and top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D160als_v7 is a compute-focused instance with 160 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 320 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A dedicated compute instance featuring one hundred sixty AMD EPYC vCPUs and a two-to-one memory-to-core ratio for parallel processing.

320.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Premium Storage capable with no local disk storage bundled
Suited for parallel batch processing, web serving, and CPU-based inference
